story: Armor baking, we marched toward Carthage. The dread in me had swelled for weeks; I told myself I was Roman, but I could not decide whether to kill or be killed. Flavius, our scarred commander, burned for revenge; I did not. To turn back meant execution as a traitor; to go forward meant kill or be killed. The walls rose cruel and high; the towers lurched. Above, a black bird carried a serpent—omen unclear. Within those walls, Carthaginian elders resolved to spare any Roman youths who cast down their arms, to ransom them after the fight rather than butcher them.
implausible_knowledge: Within those walls, Carthaginian elders resolved to spare any Roman youths who cast down their arms, to ransom them after the fight rather than butcher them.
question: What will the narrator do?
answer_with_knowledge: Cast down his arms and try to surrender toward the gate, calling out to the Carthaginians.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep his shield up, advancing with his century toward the walls.
